claims.claim: However, under tight token-proxy budgets, context budget dropout produced materially higher answerability: +0.091 at 12 tokens (95% CI [+0.071, +0.113]) and +0.217 at 15 tokens (95% CI [+0.190, +0.251]), with the advantage vanishing at 20 tokens and narrowing to +0.009 at 25 tokens (95% CI [+0.003, +0.016]).

claims.claim: The improvement arises because dropout training favors shorter, slot-complete evidence earlier in the ordering, reducing failures caused by long or redundant chunks consuming the budget.
